creator: alt2600@icloud.com
text:
SOLVED: Not a bug

So I had some time to look deeper and found the issue. checking the grass 7 build documentation I set out to check the sanity of gdal as from the log it was apparent something was wrong. I also cleared out any defaults from my options_set list in portconf config and just set my adds, so PCRE2 was used this go in gdal.

so ended up running gdal_translate per the grass docs and got an illegal instruction, which I saw in the logs too. I've felt this pain in qgis when qt5-3d saw the same avx issue being forced on.

$ lldb gdal_translate
(lldb) target create "gdal_translate"
Current executable set to 'gdal_translate' (x86_64).
(lldb) r
Process 74458 launched: '/usr/local/bin/gdal_translate' (x86_64)
Process 74458 stopped
* thread #1, name = 'gdal_translate', stop reason = signal SIGILL: privileged instruction
    frame #0: 0x000000083ccb60c4 libtiledb.so`___lldb_unnamed_symbol1342 + 4
libtiledb.so`___lldb_unnamed_symbol1342:
->  0x83ccb60c4 <+4>:  vxorps %xmm0, %xmm0, %xmm0
    0x83ccb60c8 <+8>:  vmovups %ymm0, 0x877528(%rip)
    0x83ccb60d0 <+16>: movq   $0x0, 0x87753d(%rip)
    0x83ccb60db <+27>: leaq   0x877516(%rip), %rsi
(lldb) q


vxorps is an AVX instruction and I'm building on a westmere dinosaur. It seems tiledb is implicitly enabling CFLAG -mAVX2 if the compiler supports AVX2. So I disabled tiledb, rebuilt gdal, and grass7 installed fine. going to work out qgis next. I am going to leave this open in case any feedback, and reference the pr on the tiledb pr I will craft next. looks patchable, but not a grass7 issue at all. I think this is new in tiledb maybe with 2.15.2 recently updated version, as I feel I have always had this on, but not sure. So this worked in my case
